content: ADHD is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by persistent patterns of inattention and/or hyperactivity-impulsivity interfering with functioning or development. Inattention symptoms include difficulty sustaining attention, careless mistakes, not listening, failing to follow through, disorganization, avoiding sustained mental effort, losing things, distractibility, forgetfulness. Hyperactivity-impulsivity includes fidgeting, leaving seat, running/climbing excessively, inability to play quietly, being 'on the go,' excessive talking, blurting answers, difficulty waiting turn, interrupting. Symptoms present before age 12, occur in ≥2 settings, and cause impairment. Lifetime prevalence ~5-7% in children, ~2-5% in adults. Pathophysiology involves dopamine/norepinephrine dysregulation in prefrontal-striatal circuits, high heritability (~70-80%).
